title: The Switch Statement 🚂
description: A cleaner way to write code when there are many exact options.

slides.title: When If/Else Gets Too Long 📜
Cases and Breaks 🛑
Switch in Action 💻
slides.content: Try changing the role variable to 'editor', 'user', or 'hacker' to see the switch handle the routing:

quizData.question: What happens if you forget to include the 'break' keyword at the end of a matching case?

options.text: Code executes the matching case, AND the cases below it
The switch skips the case entirely

quizData.explanation: This is known as 'fall-through'. If you don't 'break', JavaScript will keep executing all subsequent lines in the switch block, regardless of whether their case statements match!

slides.title: Switch vs If/Else 🥊
slides.content:
## When to use which?

**Use If/Else when:**
* Testing ranges (`score > 50`)
* Combining logic (`health > 0 && ammo > 0`)
* Testing different variables (`if(age>18) ... else if (hasID) ...`)

**Use Switch when:**
* Testing exactly one variable against many specific, discrete values (like menu options, days of the week, or enemy types).

Under the hood, `switch` uses strict equality (`===`), so it won't perform type coercion!

quizData.question: What keyword in a switch statement serves the exact same purpose as an 'else' block?
quizData.explanation: The 'default' keyword acts as the catch-all. If no specific case matches, the default code block will be executed.

slides.title: Chapter Summary! 🎉
slides.content:
## You Finished Chapter 4!

Your code can now make decisions! You've learned how to:

* Create branches with `if` and `else`
* Chain options with `else if`
* Flatten nested checks using `&&` and `||`
* Handle many exact matches beautifully with `switch`

Up next: Chapter 5, where we teach JavaScript how to automate boring, repetitive tasks using Loops! 🔁
